rows.source: Wikipedia contributors, "Solar Hijri calendar", section "Comparison with Gregorian calendar" (SH 1355: 21 March 1976 – 20 March 1977). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar (accessed 2026-03-20). Aligns with PSRI / institutional mapping 1 Farvardin 1355 SH = 1 Farvardin 2535 Shahanshahi in SPEC.md § Epoch.

Wikipedia contributors, "Solar Hijri calendar", section "Comparison with Gregorian calendar" (SH 1356: 21 March 1977 – 20 March 1978). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar (accessed 2026-03-20).
rows.notes: Ordinary Solar Hijri year within Shahanshahi era.

rows.source: Wikipedia contributors, "Solar Hijri calendar", section "Comparison with Gregorian calendar" (SH 1357: 21 March 1978 – 20 March 1979). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar (accessed 2026-03-20).
rows.notes: Last full Solar Hijri year overlapping Shahanshahi civil era.

rows.source: Wikipedia contributors, "Solar Hijri calendar", section "Comparison with Gregorian calendar" (SH 1355 common year, ends 20 March 1977). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar (accessed 2026-03-20). Esfand length 29 days per 1925 law as transcribed by Iranian Astronomical Society: https://www.asi.ir/fa/news/13025/%DB%8C%DA%A8%D8%B5%D8%AF%D9%85%DB%8C%D9%86-%D8%B3%D8%A7%D9%84-%D8%AA%D8%B5%D9%88%DB%8C%D8%A8-%D8%AA%D9%82%D9%88%DB%8C%D9%85-%D8%AF%D8%B1-%D8%A7%DB%8C%D8%B1%D8%A7%D9%86
rows.notes: Last civil day before 1 Farvardin 2536; ordinary-year Esfand boundary.

rows.source: Wikipedia contributors, "Solar Hijri calendar", section "Comparison with Gregorian calendar" (SH 1356 common year, ends 20 March 1978). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar (accessed 2026-03-20). Esfand 29 days per 1925 law (IAS transcription URL as row above).

Wikipedia contributors, "Solar Hijri calendar", section "Comparison with Gregorian calendar" (SH 1354* leap: 21 March 1975 – 20 March 1976). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar (accessed 2026-03-20). Leap / 30 Esfand consistent with Heydari-Malayeri mod-33 rule (arXiv:astro-ph/0409620) as summarized in SPEC.md § Mode A.
rows.notes: Proleptic Shahanshahi year 2534 (= 1354 SH); leap Esfand 30 immediately before 1 Farvardin 2535.

rows.source: Derived: 31-day months Farvardin–Tir per 1925 law (IAS transcription, URL in row shahanshahi 2535-12-29). Civil count from 1 Farvardin 1355 SH = 1976-03-21 per Wikipedia Solar Hijri comparison table (accessed 2026-03-20).
rows.notes: Month-length boundary: last day of Tir (month 4).

rows.source: Derived: same month grid and anchor as row shahanshahi 2535-04-31 (IAS + Wikipedia).
rows.notes: First day of Mehr (month 7) after 31-day Shahrivar.

rows.source: Derived: civil arithmetic from 1 Farvardin 1357 SH = 1978-03-21 (Wikipedia Solar Hijri comparison table) with 1925 month lengths (IAS transcription).
rows.notes: Illustrative mid-era boundary; SPEC documents ambiguity for last Shahanshahi-labelled civil day vs repeal date.

rows.source: Wikipedia contributors, "Solar Hijri calendar", section "Comparison with Gregorian calendar" (SH 1375* leap: 20 March 1996 – 20 March 1997). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar (accessed 2026-03-20). Shahanshahi year = 1375 + 1180 = 2555 (proleptic labelling).
rows.notes: Leap-year Nowruz on Gregorian 20 March; proleptic Shahanshahi outside legal Pahlavi era.

rows.source: Wikipedia contributors, "Solar Hijri calendar", section "Comparison with Gregorian calendar" (SH 1374 ends 19 March 1996; 1375* leap follows). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_Hijri_calendar (accessed 2026-03-20). Aligned with SPEC.md § Mode A: 1374 Hijri Shamsi has remainder 21 (mod 33) and is a **common** year — Esfand 29 days — so the last civil day is 29 Esfand, still Gregorian 19 March 1996 before 1 Farvardin 1375 on 20 March.
rows.notes: Last day of proleptic Shahanshahi 2554 (= 1374 SH) before 1 Farvardin 2555; Mode A month grid.
